items.title: Everton appeal against order to pay £35m to Burnley over PSR breach
items.summary: Everton have launched an appeal after being ordered to pay Burnley £35m in compensation after they were sued over Premier League profitability and sustainability breaches from the 2021/22 season when the Clarets were relegated.

items.title: Official: Somalian ref had suspected terror ties
items.summary: A United States official said Tuesday night that Omar Artan, the Somalian referee who was denied entry into the country ahead of the World Cup, was refused admission due to "association with suspected members of terror organizations."

items.title: USWNT gets scrappy win as Brazil sees 8 reds
items.summary: Sophia Wilson forced an own goal in the second half for the United States in a scrappy 1-0 win over Brazil in Fortaleza on Tuesday night in which the home side finished with nine players and was shown a total of eight red cards.

items.title: Poch: U.S. can win WC despite no top 100 players
items.summary: United States men's national team coach Mauricio Pochettino said his team can win the World Cup despite not having any players ranked among the top 100.

items.title: Messi scores in cameo in front of 88K in Alabama
items.summary: Argentine legend Messi, who is working his way back from muscle fatigue ahead of the start of the FIFA World Cup, came on in the 70th minute and scored from the penalty spot right after in his team's 3-0 win over Iceland.
